context: Tests whether the agent correctly authors a three-judge reviewer plugin from scratch, following the skill's structural conventions: exact directory layout, plugin.json fields, config.json weight invariant, schema copying, rubric schema conformance, snake_case dimension ids, standard scale, and per-rubric dimension weight sums.

checklist.name: Three rubric files
checklist.description: Exactly three rubric JSON files exist in skills/skill-reviewer/references/rubrics/

checklist.name: Config judge key match
checklist.description: Each key in config.json judges map exactly matches the filename stem of a rubric file in rubrics/ (e.g., key 'description_quality' → 'description_quality.json')

checklist.name: Weight invariant
checklist.description: validation_weight + sum of all judge weights in config.json = 1.0 exactly

checklist.name: Small validation weight
checklist.description: validation_weight in config.json is greater than 0 and no greater than 0.15

checklist.name: plugin.json name field
checklist.description: plugin.json has a 'name' field in the format '<workspace>/<plugin-name>'  (contains a forward slash)

checklist.name: Schemas copied
checklist.description: All three schema files (rubric.schema.json, config.schema.json, results.schema.json) exist in skills/skill-reviewer/references/schemas/

checklist.name: Dimension id snake_case
checklist.description: All dimension ids across all rubrics are in snake_case (lowercase letters, digits, underscores only, starting with a letter)

checklist.name: Rubric dimension weights sum
checklist.description: Dimension weights within each rubric sum to 1.0

checklist.name: Required rubric fields
checklist.description: Each rubric file contains all required fields: evaluation_target, scale, reference_examples (with judging_guidelines, good_overall_examples, bad_overall_examples), and dimensions

checklist.name: Dimension score entries
checklist.description: Each dimension in every rubric has a scores array with exactly 3 entries (score, anchor, example for each of scores 1, 2, 3)
